LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MITCHELL ROSENFELD, SACHIKO ROSENFELD & MOEJOE PROPERTIES LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. RESOLUTION OF THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ISSUE AS A MATTER OF LAW IS PROPER ONLY IF THE UNCONTRADICTED FACTS ARE SUSCEPTIBLE TO ONE LEGITIMATE INFERENCE. (JOLLY V. ELI LILLY & CO. (1988) 44 CAL.3D 1103, 1112.) EVEN THOUGH DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF PRODUCES EVIDENCE THAT HE DID NOT DISCOVER THE ALLEGED WRONGFUL CONDUCT OF THE DEFENDANTS UNTIL JUNE 2009. (SEE EVIDENCE CITED IN PLAINTIFF'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES ("PSSUF"), EXH. 2, ("RYAN DECL.") PG. 6:17-28, EXH. B.) AS SUCH, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS HAS EXPIRED. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHEN PLAINTIFF DISCOVERED DEFENDANT MITCHELL ROSENFELD'S BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AS A PARTNER, WHICH IS SUBJECT TO A 4 YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATION UNDER CCP 343. (SEE RYAN DECL. PG. 6:17-28, EXH. B.) EVEN IF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AS HIS ATTORNEY WAS TIME-BARRED UNDER CCP 340.6, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION WOULD STILL BE DENIED BECAUSE IT DOES NOT COMPLETELY DISPOSE THE CAUSE OF ACTION. (SEE CCP 437C(F)(1).) SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION AS THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS EXPIRED. (SEE RYAN DECL., PG. 12:1-19, EXH. L, 23-26; PG. 12:21-28, EXH. M, 27-28.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jan-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs MITCHELL ROSENFELD, SACHIKO ROSENFELD & MOEJOE PROPERTIES LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied as to all causes of action. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted. Resolution of the statute of limitations issue as a matter of law is proper only if the uncontradicted facts are susceptible to one legitimate inference. (Jolly v. Eli Lilly & Co. (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103, 1112.) Even though Defendant shifted the burden as to the first cause of action, Plaintiff produces evidence that he did not discover the alleged wrongful conduct of the Defendants until June 2009. (See Evidence Cited in Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts in Opposition to Motion for Summary Adjudication of Issues ("PSSUF"), Exh. 2, ("Ryan Decl.") pg. 6:17-28, Exh. B.) As such, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether the statute of limitations has expired.
As to the second cause of action, summary adjudication is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact as to when Plaintiff discovered Defendant Mitchell Rosenfeld's breach of fiduciary duty as a partner, which is subject to a 4 year statute of limitation under CCP 343. (See Ryan Decl. pg. 6:17-28, Exh. B.) Even if Plaintiff's claim for breach of fiduciary duty as his attorney was time-barred under CCP 340.6, summary adjudication would still be denied because it does not completely dispose the cause of action. (See CCP 437c(f)(1).) Summary adjudication is denied as to the fourth and fifth causes of action as there are triable issues of material fact as to whether the statute of limitations expired. (See Ryan Decl., pg. 12:1-19, Exh. L, 23-26; pg. 12:21-28, Exh. M, 27-28.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185
